description: Asset Factory - Hybrid asset management for scene editor

userStories.title: Add API endpoint to read assets.json
userStories.description: Create a read-only endpoint that returns the flattened assets.json structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Response includes dot-notation keys and asset definitions
userStories.notes: Add to scene-editor/vite.config.ts alongside existing endpoints

userStories.title: Add API endpoint to write assets.json
userStories.description: Create an endpoint to save modified assets back to assets.json.
userStories.notes: Must convert flat dot-notation back to nested structure for storage

userStories.title: Create Asset Factory tab shell
userStories.description: Add a new tab to the scene editor for asset management.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Assets' tab appears in scene editor navigation
userStories.notes: Check existing tab structure in scene-editor/src/components/

userStories.title: Display asset list in Asset Factory
userStories.description: Show all assets from assets.json in a browsable list.
acceptanceCriteria: Displays assets grouped by category (environments, characters, ui, etc.)
Shows asset key, type, and texture for each

userStories.title: View/edit asset details
userStories.description: Click an asset to view and edit its properties.
acceptanceCriteria: Shows all properties: type, texture, origin, scale, depth, animations

userStories.title: Create new asset from texture
userStories.description: Allow creating a new asset definition from an existing texture.
acceptanceCriteria: Texture picker shows available textures from textures.json
User specifies: dot-notation path, type, origin, scale
userStories.notes: Auto-suggest dot-notation path based on texture name

userStories.title: Build reverse texture-to-asset lookup
userStories.description: Create utility to find which asset uses a given texture key.
acceptanceCriteria: Function: findAssetByTexture(textureKey) returns asset path or null
Handles multiple assets using same texture (returns first/all)

userStories.title: Add save-time validation for unregistered assets
userStories.description: When saving a scene, check if any elements use unregistered textures.
acceptanceCriteria: On save, iterate elements and check each asset against assets.json
Identify elements using texture keys not mapped to any asset
If all assets registered, save proceeds normally
If unregistered found, show validation dialog (next story)

userStories.title: Create unregistered asset dialog
userStories.description: Dialog that appears when saving with unregistered assets.
acceptanceCriteria: Lists unregistered textures with suggested dot-notation paths
User can select existing asset (duplicate detection)
'Create & Save' button creates assets then saves scene

userStories.title: Write scene elements to scenes.json
userStories.description: When saving, also write element definitions to scenes.json using dot-notation.
acceptanceCriteria: Converts texture keys to dot-notation asset paths
userStories.notes: This is the key change that makes editor scenes render in game

userStories.title: Integration test: Create scene in editor, verify in game
userStories.description: End-to-end verification that editor scenes now work in game.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new scene in editor with one background image
Save the scene (creates asset if needed)
Load game and navigate to new scene
Verify in browser (both editor and game)
userStories.notes: Final verification story. Test scene 'AssetFactoryTestScene' created with manual browser verification procedure documented in progress.txt
